title: Menu Item Triggers
text: Menu Item Triggers Menu items have associated triggers in which to program the conditions under which they are displayed and the action they perform. Table 1. Menu Triggers Trigger Available On Description trigger preDisplay Option Dynamic menu Cascading menu Separator Drop-down menu Right Align Marker The preDisplay trigger is activated before a menu is displayed. You can control if and how the menu item is displayed using the ProcScript functions $hide , $disable , $check , and $inlinemenu , which are only available in this trigger. You can use the preDisplay trigger to set or return values and to specify if an item is available. For example, you can use the preDisplay trigger, to enable or disable the item, or to check or uncheck it depending on the current state of the application. trigger option Option Dynamic menu The option trigger is activated when the user clicks the menu item or presses the shortcut key. You can use the option trigger to program an action, such as activating a form. Related Links ProcScript for Menus You control how a menu item is displayed by using the ProcScript instructions in the preDisplay trigger. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
